item.title: It's another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
about to brew a truck driver... greetings online friends
i want that jacket now also
i also prefer crunch water but because of the cold
not all restaurants provide it
muthaflippin icons
mmm hmm
i wonder what it tastes like
we all remember the 'snake scene'
this little fucker is a cheater
just a city boy
i can taste these. oh man they were so good
it has to also be the exact shade of blue seen in the video
everyone's favorite weapon
typical Michael
yes
dang
legitimate question
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: In 2003 I had to sell my guitar to make rent…I bought it back two days ago!
item.description:
I think I am on a bit of a journey to redeem my past… #guitar #memes #uplifting #happy
Gibson Gothic Les Paul

item.dc:creator: Kairuf

item.title: Phone clearing dump Thursday (I need the space for you know what)
item.description:
1. Congrats on the new daddies!
2. Pacino too! (had a baby in 23 with his lady)
3. How is she keeping a straight face
4
5. Can we get capes this time? They look so fuckin awesome!
6. Who here is still playing it
7
8
9
10. Ouch
11. Photo of an uncontacted tribe in the Amazon (no phone in sight)
12
13
14. Abuna Yem’ata Guh | Tigray Region | Ethiopia Situated at a height of 2,580 metres (8,460 ft), it has to be climbed on foot to reach. It is notable for its architecture, dome & wall paintings dating back to the 5th century.
15. And for the 3 people that made it to the bottom, I would like to share with you that I'm making a thing. And that thing is a game, to be played on your magical desk rectangle thingy. The game is kinda like "The Stanley Parable" but worse, BUT if you enjoyed the humor in this dump, my A.I. algorithm has detected a 0,034% chance that you will like the humor in the game too. Anyway, I'm rambling, wishlist on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Starting martial arts again (hopefully)
item.description: Just found a place that's even closer to me than I thought that teaches jodo, or the Japanese style of fighting using a 4 foot staff. I've wanted to try weaponry martial arts ever since I got my black belt in Tae Kwon Do and this is the first viable opportunity I've found. Wish me luck! Most Viral Edit: Brah. I have like 84 upvotes. That is not front page material. What is wrong with your algorithm? Or are you just that desperate to pretend this place isn't slowy dying off . . .
item.dc:creator: Thisisallyourfault

item.title: For those who could use a helping hand <3
item.description:
Life is hard. Especially in times like these. I wanted to take a second to share a collection of resources for anyone who needs them. Unfortunately, this is mostly exclusive to the US, because that’s where I live. I’m sorry about that. :(
Healthcare.gov’s website on healthcare when unemployed: https://www.healthcare.gov/unemployed/ Healthcare.gov’s directory to find an affordable healthcare center near you: https://www.healthcare.gov/community-health-centers/ Health Partners’ website on how to get affordable healthcare if you don’t qualify for Medicaid: https://www.healthpartners.com/blog/cant-afford-health-insurance-dont-qualify-for-medicaid/ Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services’ Resources for the Uninsured: https://www.hhs.gov/guidance/sites/default/files/hhs-guidance-documents/Resources%20for%20the%20Uninsured%20FINAL%20508.pdf KHealth gives eight resources for those that are currently not insured at all, or underinsured: https://khealth.com/blog/healthcare/resources-for-people-currently-uninsured-or-underinsured/
US Department of Health and Human Resources list of caregiver resources: https://www.hhs.gov/programs/providers-and-facilities/resources-for-caregivers/index.html Family Caregiver Alliance’s home caregiving guide: https://www.caregiver.org/resource/caregiving-home-guide-community-resources/ CaringInfo’s website on caregiving resources and links: https://www.caringinfo.org/planning/caregiving/caregiving-resources/ Family Caregiver Alliance’s website that links to all sorts of resources for caregivers: https://www.caregiver.org/caregiver-resources/ Vitas Healthcare’s Website on Caregiver Burnout: https://www.vitas.com/family-and-caregiver-support/caregiving/caregiver-life-balance/signs-of-caregiver-burnout-and-how-to-prevent-it CaringBridge’s Website on caregiver burnout: https://www.caringbridge.org/resources/how-can-a-caregiver-experience-freedom-from-burnout/?gad_source=1&gclid=CjwKCAjwkuqvBhAQEiwA65XxQL950pxrTge5wHOB7pktoVL_reDTDBerM7cE1tqXmqWsSGWTl-5CPBoCL3UQAvD_BwE
CHILD ABUSE: American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry’s child abuse and resource center: https://www.aacap.org/AACAP/Families_and_Youth/Resource_Centers/Child_Abuse_Resource_Center/Home.aspx The CDC’s list of child abuse and neglect resources: https://www.cdc.gov/violenceprevention/childabuseandneglect/resources.html . . DOMESTIC VIOLENCE: The National Domestic Violence Website: https://www.thehotline.org/ The extra good thing about this website is that it also lets you exit quickly without being tracked if you’re in a situation where your internet use is being tracked by your partner . . SEXUAL VIOLENCE: National Sexual Violence Resource Center: https://www.nsvrc.org/ RAINN sexual violence resources: https://www.rainn.org/resources . . HUMAN TRAFFICKING: The US Department of Justice has a website of resources for human trafficking victims: https://www.justice.gov/humantrafficking/resources There is an international human trafficking program called RAPHA. I decided to include it because it’s a well known program for trafficking, but I’m not sure that I would trust it. The problem is that they use a “holistic approach” to victim aftercare treatment, which means that whatever treatment they give is not medically regulated, and might not involve trained mental health professionals with a certified license to practice. Link: https://rapha.org/ The US Government’s “Blue Campaign” for human trafficking victims: https://www.dhs.gov/blue-campaign?utm_source=google.com&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=rg.search-fy24.blue.national-english&utm_content=location&utm_term=human%20trafficking%20in&gad_source=1&gclid=CjwKCAjwkuqvBhAQEiwA65XxQFbbi87FCUg1bSpjsE3Gltl0fDoEVODyqDHovkHcU19I8duYlSdFsxoCMEgQAvD_BwE . . MISC: Need to make a police report? I’ve been there. Here’s my guide on making a police report to make it as smooth as possible: https://imgur.com/gallery/D2bkKu1
PFLAG’s list of resources for struggling LGBTQ+ people: https://pflag.org/resource/support-hotlines/ More LGBTQ+ Crisis resources: https://ostem.org/page/crisis-hotlines Thrive Hotline for LGBTQ+ resources: https://thrivelifeline.org/index.html
Full List of Substance Abuse Hotlines: https://www.help.org/drug-abuse-hotline/ Gamblers Anonymous: https://www.gamblersanonymous.org/ga/ Narcotics Anonymous: https://m.na.org/ Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA)’s list of resources, including a helpline: https://www.samhsa.gov/find-help/national-helpline
US Department of Veteran Affairs’ Help for Veterans Link: https://www.ptsd.va.gov/gethelp/help_for_veterans.asp# . Wounded Warrior Project’s Care Network for those with PTSD, traumatic brain injury (TBI), military sexual trauma (MST), and disabilities that may have been sustained during service Link: https://www.woundedwarriorproject.org/programs/warrior-care-network . Wounded Warrior Project Chatline for Crisis Link: https://www.woundedwarriorproject.org/programs/wwp-talk . Project Odyssey, a no-cost 12 week mental health program for veterans Link: https://www.woundedwarriorproject.org/programs/project-odyssey . Veterans Crisis Line Link: https://www.veteranscrisisline.net/ . US Department of Veteran Affairs Assistance for Homelessness Link 1: https://www.va.gov/homeless/ Link 2: https://www.va.gov/homeless/resources.asp . Mental Health Support For Women Veterans Link: https://www.va.gov/womenvet/?gclid=Cj0KCQjwmtGjBhDhARIsAEqfDEfPP0ufVlwOSsWKzGVq59VicNDtg-IdMZANgaG28DS8nOVVaIMW18caAjCsEALw_wcB . Veteran Suicide Prevention Website Link: https://www.va.gov/health-care/health-needs-conditions/mental-health/suicide-prevention/
The official HIV government website has a page dedicated to any suicidal thoughts or emotional crisis that might come with a positive diagnosis: https://www.hiv.gov/blog/lifeline-and-988 The CDC website has a page on HIV consultation and referral services: https://www.cdc.gov/hiv/library/hotlines.html Ryan White HIV/AIDS program's list of HIV hotlines for every state in the US: https://ryanwhite.hrsa.gov/hiv-care/hotlines The State of New York has an entire program dedicated to HIV/AIDS testing, resources, and mental health counseling: https://www.gmhc.org/at-a-glance/
